text.title: Project Bootstrap Pipeline
text.summary: Seven-phase pipeline from idea to TASK_000, with mandatory Phase 4 user confirmation and the output artifact structure under .aidocs/.

section.title: Pipeline Phases
section.p: Phase sequence: 1. Vision Brainstorm (Five Whys to real need) → 2. Tech Stack Selection (frontend, backend, database) → 3. MVP Definition (max 3-5 features, cut 50% nice-to-haves) → 4. User Confirmation (MANDATORY — present summary, get explicit approval) → 5. Backlog Creation (feature specs with FR/AC) → 6. Constitution (write project constitution) → 7. TASK_000 (setup task with project goals).

rule.statement: Phase 4 user confirmation cannot be skipped or simulated — the bootstrap pipeline must hard-fail with NEEDS-HUMAN if no explicit approval token is received.
rule.rationale: LLMs eager to complete will simulate user approval; bootstrap must detect non-interactive mode and exit rather than invent answers. An unsigned constitution is not a constitution.

rule.statement: Bootstrap must detect non-interactive mode (cron, CI) and exit with FAIL needs-human; do not invent answers.
rule.rationale: The AskUserQuestion step blocks in non-interactive runs; an agent that invents user approval produces a constitution nobody agreed to, corrupting the entire project record.

section.title: Backlog Creation Rules

rule.statement: Every spec must have a RICE row (reach/impact/confidence/effort frontmatter) before leaving backlog; reject specs without RICE at grooming time.
rule.rationale: Unscored backlog items cannot be prioritized; RICE without frontmatter is invisible to the rice-reorder script and the sprint kickoff agent.

rule.statement: Agents must refuse to invent features not present in the idea-validation or competitive-analysis inputs.
rule.rationale: MLP hallucination — inventing WOW features without competitor evidence — is the most common failure mode; the PM's job is to make evidence the precondition, not the review step.

section.reference: RICE-sort the backlog and write a ranked INDEX.md

rule.statement: When two PM agents may run concurrently, take a flock on .aidocs/.numbering.lock before assigning the next feature number or task number.
rule.rationale: Concurrent agents assigning {NN} without a lock create numbering collisions that break spec filenames and all downstream references.
